title: Detecting Bridges in a Spanning Tree: Fix the Low Link Values

statement.markdown: Given a connected undirected graph with n nodes and m edges, and a spanning tree of n-1 edges, you are required to find all the bridges in this graph. A bridge is an edge in the graph whose removal increases the number of connected components in the graph. You should return a boolean array where the presence of an edge is determined by the corresponding value in the array.
statement.inputFormat:
The input format is as follows:

First line: The number of vertices \(n\)

Second line: \(n-1\), the number of edges in the spanning tree
statement.outputFormat:
The output format is as follows:

 boolean array representing whether each edge is a bridge or not
statement.notes: In order to correctly determine the bridges, the time complexity of the code should be O(m + n) where m is the number of edges and n is the number of vertices.

examples.explanation: There is a tree with edges [(0, 1), (1, 2), (2, 3), (3, 4)]. The edges (1, 2), (3, 4) are bridges.
There is a tree with edges [(0, 1), (1, 2), (2, 3)]. The edges (0, 1), (1, 2) are bridges.

functionSignature.cpp: bool isBridge(int u, int v, vector<vector<int>>& graph, vector<int>& disc, vector<int>& low, vector<bool>& visited, int& time);
functionSignature.java: boolean isBridge(int u, int v, List<List<Integer>> graph, List<Integer> disc, List<Integer> low, List<Boolean> visited, int time);
functionSignature.python: def isBridge(self, u: int, v: int, graph: List[List[int]], disc: List[int], low: List[int], visited: List[bool], time: int) -> bool;
functionSignature.javascript: function isBridge(u, v, graph, disc, low, visited, time): boolean;
